issues.display_title: Issue #1468: Add GoThru API NEW Collection to Bruno
issues.main_description_html:
RESULT 30/06/26

✅ Implementation: GoThru API NEW using Bruno Collection

Overview

Successfully implemented and exported the Bruno API collection for "GoThru API NEW". The collection is now structured using standard YAML files and is version-controlled within the repository. This allows any team member to easily import, test, and collaborate on the API endpoints.

Details of Changes

1. Directory Structure

Created the main collection directory at:
api-collection/GoThru API NEW/
The root contains the opencollection.yml file which defines the Bruno collection metadata and ignores unnecessary folders (like node_modules and .git).

2. Environments Setup

Configured two distinct environments to switch between production and local development easily:

  • Live (environments/Live.yml): Sets the base_url variable to https://apil.gothru.co.
  • Local - Dev (environments/Local - Dev.yml): Sets the base_url variable to http://0.0.0.0:8080.

3. Endpoints and Modules

The API endpoints have been categorized into the following core modules:

User Module

  • User setting.yml, User detail.yml, Update User.yml, Update Storage.yml, etc.
  • Tours Sub-module: A massive sub-folder containing operations for tours.
    • Publishing: Endpoints for publishing tours, setting place IDs, validating publishing, etc.
    • Stitching: Image uploading, downloading ZIP files, storing stitching details, and deleting stitch files.
    • Street View Publishing: Moderation, relinking, mapping approvals, deleting published tours, and panorama management (Pano info, deleting extra panos, etc.).
    • Walkthrough Video: Video requests, getting video modes, deleting projects, etc.
    • Tour Management: Renaming, searching, splitting, and transferring tours.

Worker Module

  • Auto Moderator.yml setup for background worker tasks.

Logs Module

  • Store Activity.yml, get list files.yml, get file content.yml, Delete file.yml for activity logging and log file management.

Conclusion

The API collection is now fully available and ready for use. By leveraging Bruno's text-based file structure (YAML), all requests, headers, and body payloads are cleanly formatted and can be easily reviewed in pull requests going forward.

Objective

Integrate the complete "GoThru API NEW" collection into the repository using the Bruno API client format. This gives the team a centralized, version-controlled, and easily accessible set of API endpoints for development, testing, and debugging.

Requirements

  • API Collection Setup
    • Initialize a new Bruno API collection in the repository under api-collection/GoThru API NEW.
    • Ensure the root configuration (opencollection.yml) is correctly set up.
  • Environments Configuration
    • Add a Live environment pointing to the production server.
    • Add a Local - Dev environment pointing to the local development server.
  • Endpoint Categorization
    • Organize endpoints into logical folders: User, Tours, Worker, logs.
    • Within Tours, further categorize into Publishing, Stitching, Moderation, Panoramas, Walkthrough Video.
  • Request Details
    • Each endpoint must include the appropriate HTTP method, URL (using environment variables), headers (e.g. Authorization), and request body payloads where applicable.
  • Version Control
    • All collection files saved in YAML format to facilitate easy change tracking via Git.

Acceptance Criteria

  1. The Bruno client can successfully load the GoThru API NEW collection from the repository.
  2. Environments can be switched seamlessly between Live and Local - Dev.
  3. All predefined endpoints are present and formatted correctly.

Source: docs/bruno_api_task.md

issues.display_title: Issue #1469: Skip Whitelabel URL for Google Stats (API side) for Alin Accounts
issues.main_description_html:
Description

API-side counterpart of dashboard task GoThruMedia/gothru-dashboard#4109. The Google Stats logic lives on GoThru API, so the URL/whitelabel handling for these stats needs to be addressed here.

Feedback note from an internal/owner account (user_id: 2):

"As a note, for user_id=2 (me) there is no need to use the whitelabel URL for Google Stats."

Currently the Google Stats are built using the whitelabel URL. For this account (and similar internal/owner accounts), the whitelabel URL is unnecessary — the stats should use the direct / default (non-whitelabel) URL instead.

API Endpoint

GET /portfolios/gstats?cid={cid} → app/Http/Controllers/User/Portfolio/Actions/GetGoogleStatsPortfolio.php

  • Auth required (middleware injects user_id, subscribtion)
  • Validates cid is present
  • Checks subscription against allowed list
  • Looks up session_id from google_plus table via user_id + cid
  • Builds link: https://gstats.co/stats_{session_id} (default)
  • For agency accounts: overrides base URL with whitelabel domain from white_label table (apply_all=1, default=true)

Scope (API)

  • In the GoThru API Google Stats logic, do not apply the whitelabel URL for user_id: 2 (and any other accounts that fit the agreed rule).
  • Return / use the direct/default stats URL for these accounts instead of the whitelabel domain.
  • Leave whitelabel behavior unchanged for all other accounts that should keep it.
  • Coordinate with the dashboard change (#4109) so frontend + API are consistent.

Acceptance Criteria

  1. For user_id: 2, the Google Stats served by the API no longer use the whitelabel URL — they use the direct/default URL.
  2. Whitelabel URL behavior remains intact for all other (non-exempt) accounts.

❓ Open question (confirm before implementation): Should this exemption apply only to user_id: 2, or to a broader group (e.g. all internal/admin/owner accounts, or accounts without whitelabel configured)?

Note from Developer

Summary of Changes

  • Added an owner account guard in GetGoogleStatsPortfolio.php — when user_id equals 2, the whitelabel URL lookup is skipped entirely and the default https://gstats.co base URL is used.
  • No changes to the whitelabel flow for all other accounts.

⚠️ Unable to provide a demo — no sample tour available to test the full Google Stats URL generation flow end-to-end.

Related: GoThruMedia/gothru-dashboard#4109 (dashboard side)

issues.display_title: Issue #1345: Migrate Get Unlock Publish Price
issues.main_description_html:
Migrate this endpoint to the Domain-Based ADR architecture.

Endpoint: GET /products/unlock-publish
Current code: Payment\Stripe\OneTimeController@priceUnlockPublish
Why it's simple: ownership + cost calc

Category A — thin logic currently written inline in a large controller. Extract into Controller → Action → Repository with a DTO and exception mapping.

Reference: docs/ADR_MIGRATION_CANDIDATES.md

Note from Developer

Migrated OneTimeController@priceUnlockPublish to strict Domain-Based ADR via PR #1465.

Changes:

  • Controller GetUnlockPublishPriceController@handle — validates mid/type (422)
  • Action GetUnlockPublishPriceAction — ownership + billing gates, publish flat $14, publish_blueline cost formula (incremental / flat / per-pano)
  • DTO UnlockPublishPriceDto
  • Exceptions NotTourOwnerException, BillingInfoRequiredException, InvalidUnlockTypeException (all 409) + mapper entries
  • ToursClass / BillingClass / ConstellationClass injected into Action (HTTP-free, mockable)
  • 7 unit tests added (GetUnlockPublishPriceActionTest — not-owner, no-billing, invalid-type, publish flat, blue-line incremental, blue-line >150, basic-tier flat)

Breaking change: Response key renamed from prduct_name → product_name (typo fix). Clients reading prduct_name must update.

API Doc: docs/v2/payment/get-unlock-publish-price.md

issues.display_title: Issue #1346: Migrate Get Billing Card Details
issues.main_description_html:
Migrate this endpoint to the Domain-Based ADR architecture.

Endpoint: GET /billing/card-details
Current code: Payment\Stripe\SubscriptionController@getCardDetails
Why it's simple: fetch user + Stripe card + encrypt

Category A — thin logic currently written inline in a large controller. Extract into Controller → Action → Repository with a DTO and exception mapping.

Reference: docs/ADR_MIGRATION_CANDIDATES.md

Note from Developer

Migrated SubscriptionController@getCardDetails to strict Domain-Based ADR via PR #1463.

Changes:

  • Controller GetCardDetailsController@handle — builds DTO from injected user_data.email + is_photo_studio
  • Action GetCardDetailsAction — user lookup, recurring-profile selection, test-mode resolution
  • Repository CardRepository (extends PaymentDatabaseRepository) — DB recurring-profile read + Stripe subscription→customer→default-source card
  • DTO GetCardDetailsDto
  • Exceptions UserNotFoundException (reused), SubscriptionNotFoundException (new, 409) + mapper entry
  • 5 unit tests added (GetCardDetailsActionTest — user-not-found, empty profile, non-sub profile, success, photo-studio+test-mode)

Behavior change: Legacy mapped all errors (including Stripe/infra) to 409. Domain failures stay 409; unexpected Stripe/infra errors now surface as 500/503 via the shared throwable handler.

API Doc: docs/v2/payment/get-billing-card-details.md

Migrate this endpoint to the Domain-Based ADR architecture.

Endpoint: POST /billing/edit-card
Current code: Payment\Stripe\SubscriptionController@editCardCustomer
Why it's simple: token + user + update card

Category A — thin logic currently written inline in a large controller. Extract into Controller → Action → Repository with a DTO and exception mapping.

Reference: docs/ADR_MIGRATION_CANDIDATES.md

Note from Developer

Migrated SubscriptionController@editCardCustomer to strict Domain-Based ADR via PR #1464.

Changes:

  • Controller EditCardController@handle — validates stripe_token (422)
  • Action EditCardAction — user lookup, recurring-profile guard, customer resolution, card update
  • Repository CardRepository — reuses getUserCardInfo; adds resolveCustomerId (sub_/cus_) + updateCardSource
  • DTO EditCardDto
  • Reuses UserNotFoundException + SubscriptionNotAllowedException
  • 5 unit tests added (EditCardActionTest — user-not-found, empty profile, unresolvable customer, sub update, cus + test-mode)

Behavior change: Legacy mapped Stripe ApiErrorException to 409. Domain failures stay 409; unexpected Stripe/infra errors now surface as 500/503 via the shared throwable handler.

API Doc: docs/v2/payment/edit-billing-card.md

Migrate this endpoint to the Domain-Based ADR architecture.

Endpoint: POST /coupon/delete
Current code: Payment\Stripe\CouponController@deleteCoupon
Why it's simple: token + Stripe + Redis delete

Category A — thin logic currently written inline in a large controller. Extract into Controller → Action → Repository with a DTO and exception mapping.

Reference: docs/ADR_MIGRATION_CANDIDATES.md

Note from Developer

Migrated CouponController@deleteCoupon to strict Domain-Based ADR via PR #1462.

Changes:

  • Controller DeleteCouponController@handle — 422 guard for missing coupon_id
  • Action DeleteCouponAction — admin gate + delete
  • Repository CouponRepository::deleteCoupon — Stripe coupons->delete + Redis coupon:{id} / coupons:list cleanup
  • DTO DeleteCouponDto
  • Reuses CouponAdminValidator + CouponAccessDeniedException from coupon-list migration
  • 3 unit tests added (DeleteCouponActionTest — deny non-admin, delete live, delete test-mode)

API Doc: docs/v2/payment/delete-coupon.md

Migrate this endpoint to the Domain-Based ADR architecture.

Endpoint: GET /coupon/list
Current code: Payment\Stripe\CouponController@listCoupon
Why it's simple: token + Stripe list + filter

Category A — thin logic currently written inline in a large controller. Extract into Controller → Action → Repository with a DTO and exception mapping.

Reference: docs/ADR_MIGRATION_CANDIDATES.md

Note from Developer

Migrated CouponController@listCoupon to strict Domain-Based ADR via PR #1461.

Changes:

  • Controller ListCouponController@handle — builds DTO from injected user_data + test_mode/limit/active
  • Action ListCouponAction — admin gate via validator, list, optional active filter (re-indexed)
  • Repository CouponRepository::listCoupons — Stripe coupons->all
  • Validator CouponAdminValidator (reusable admin gate, shared with delete)
  • Exception CouponAccessDeniedException (401) + mapper entry
  • 4 unit tests added (ListCouponActionTest — deny non-admin, list all, active filter, inactive filter)

API Doc: docs/v2/payment/get-coupon-list.md

Migrate this endpoint to the Domain-Based ADR architecture.

Endpoint: GET /coupon/detail/{coupon_id}
Current code: Payment\Stripe\CouponController@detailCoupon
Why it's simple: token + Stripe detail

Category A — thin logic currently written inline in a large controller. Extract into Controller → Action → Repository with a DTO and exception mapping.

Reference: docs/ADR_MIGRATION_CANDIDATES.md

Note from Developer

Migrated CouponController@detailCoupon to strict Domain-Based ADR via PR #1460.

Changes:

  • Controller GetCouponDetailController@handle — {coupon_id} param, builds DTO from injected user_data
  • Action GetCouponDetailAction — HTTP-free; test-mode resolution + redemption rules
  • Repository CouponRepository — Stripe coupon gateway (wraps StripeClass, no SQL)
  • DTO GetCouponDetailDto
  • Exceptions CouponNotFoundException (404), CouponNotRedeemableException (410) + mapper entries
  • 4 unit tests added (GetCouponDetailActionTest — not-found, exhausted→410, test-mode client, normalized fields)

Behavior fix: Legacy mapped all errors (including the redemption check) to 404, making the intended 410 "no longer redeemable" unreachable. Action now correctly separates 404 (unresolved coupon) vs 410 (redemptions exhausted).

API Doc: docs/v2/payment/get-coupon-detail.md

Migrate this endpoint to the Domain-Based ADR architecture.

Endpoint: POST /support/stop-publish
Current code: Support\SupportController@stopPublish
Why it's simple: 1 update

Category A — thin logic currently written inline in a large controller. Extract into Controller → Action → Repository with a DTO and exception mapping.

Reference: docs/ADR_MIGRATION_CANDIDATES.md

Note from Developer

Migrated Support\SupportController@stopPublish to strict Domain-Based ADR via PR #1466.

Changes:

  • Controller StopPublishController@handle — validates mid (422)
  • Action StopPublishAction — super-user gate (user_type === 2 and user_id ∈ SUPER_USER)
  • Repository SupportRepository::markUnpublished — moderation.audit.published = '2' (first write-side Support repo)
  • DTO StopPublishDto
  • Exception ForbiddenActionException (SUPPORT.FORBIDDEN → 403) + mapper entry
  • 3 unit tests added (StopPublishActionTest — wrong user_type, non-super user, authorized)

Behavior change: Legacy forbidden gate returned 409; now correctly returns 403. Input validation moves 409 → 422.

API Doc: docs/v2/support/stop-publish.md

Migrate this endpoint to the Domain-Based ADR architecture.

Endpoint: GET /billing/info
Current code: User\BillingController@getBillingInfo
Why it's simple: token + lookup + encrypt

Category A — thin logic currently written inline in a large controller. Extract into Controller → Action → Repository with a DTO and exception mapping.

Reference: docs/ADR_MIGRATION_CANDIDATES.md

Note from Developer

Migrated User\BillingController@getBillingInfo to strict Domain-Based ADR via PR #1467.

Changes:

  • Controller GetBillingInfoController@handle — builds DTO from injected user_data.user_id
  • Action GetBillingInfoAction — returns { billing: row|null }
  • Repository UserRepository::getBillingAddressByUserId (connection gothru_mod, table billing_addresses)
  • DTO GetBillingInfoDto
  • Encrypted response preserved (encryptResult); missing address returns billing: null (matches legacy)
  • 2 unit tests added (GetBillingInfoActionTest — row present, none)

API Doc: docs/v2/user/get-billing-info.md
